q.en: What color is the Hope Diamond?
q.zh: 希望钻石是什么颜色的？

options.en: Clear, like glass according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 透明，像玻璃（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says, "But me, I am deep blue, like the ocean."
explain.zh: 书中写道：“但我，是深蓝色的，像海洋一样。”

q.en: Where was the Hope Diamond first found?
q.zh: 希望钻石最初是在哪里被发现的？

options.en: In a diamond mine in India
options.zh: 在印度的一座钻石矿里

options.en: In the United States according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 在美国（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says, "People first found me in a diamond mine in India."
explain.zh: 书中说：“人们最初在印度的一座钻石矿里发现了我。”

q.en: Who first bought the stone that would become the Hope Diamond?
q.zh: 是谁最先买下后来成为希望钻石的那块石头？

options.en: An American scientist according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一位美国科学家（按书中另一种说法）
法国商人让-巴蒂斯特·塔韦尼耶

explain.en: The book says, "A French merchant, Jean-Baptiste Tavernier, first bought the stone that would become the Hope Diamond."
explain.zh: 书中说：“法国商人让-巴蒂斯特·塔韦尼耶最先买下了那块后来成为希望钻石的石头。”

q.en: Why is the diamond called the 'Hope' Diamond?
q.zh: 这颗钻石为什么叫“希望”钻石？

options.en: Because it was found by hope according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 因为它是被“希望”发现的（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Because a man named Henry Philip Hope bought it
options.zh: 因为一位名叫亨利·菲利普·霍普的人买下了它

explain.en: The book says, "a man named Henry Philip Hope bought me. He lived in England, and he is why I am called the Hope Diamond."
explain.zh: 书中说：“一位名叫亨利·菲利普·霍普的人买下了我。他住在英国，我就是因他而得名为希望钻石。”

q.en: About how much did the diamond weigh at first, and after France cut it in 1668?
q.zh: 这颗钻石最初大约多重？1668年法国切割后呢？

options.en: More than 100 carats at first; a little over 67 carats after the cut
options.zh: 最初超过100克拉；切割后略多于67克拉

options.en: 1,000 carats; then 500 carats according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 1000克拉；后来500克拉（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says it "weighed more than 100 carats" at first, and after the 1668 cut "I was a little heavier than 67 carats, a loss of about one-third of my weight."
explain.zh: 书中说它最初“重逾100克拉”，1668年切割后“略重于67克拉，损失了约三分之一的重量”。

q.en: What happened to the diamond in 1792, and what was it like after that?
q.zh: 1792年这颗钻石发生了什么？之后变成了什么样？

options.en: It turned from blue to red
It was given to a king as a gift according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 它被作为礼物送给国王（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: It was stolen, found in England, cut again to about 45 carats (size of a quarter)
options.zh: 它被偷走，在英国被找回，再被切割到约45克拉（硬币大小）

options.en: It was lost at sea forever

explain.en: The book says, "in 1792, I was stolen!... I was found in England. The bad news was that I had been cut again. I weighed about 45 carats... I was down to about the size of a quarter."
explain.zh: 书中说：“1792年，我被偷走了！……我在英国被找回。坏消息是我又被切割了一次。我重约45克拉……缩到了大约一枚25分硬币的大小。”

q.en: Who bought the diamond in 1949 and later gave it to the Smithsonian Institution?
q.zh: 谁在1949年买下钻石，后来又把它捐给了史密森学会？

options.en: Harry Winston (gave it to the Smithsonian in 1958)
options.zh: 哈里·温斯顿（1958年捐给史密森学会）

options.en: Evalyn McLean according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 伊芙琳·麦克林（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says, "Then I was sold again, to Harry Winston, in 1949" and "In 1958, Mr. Winston gave me to the Smithsonian Institution."
explain.zh: 书中说：“然后我又被卖出，1949年卖给了哈里·温斯顿”，并“1958年，温斯顿先生把我捐给了史密森学会。”

q.en: When the diamond says it is 'still blue,' what does it really mean?
q.zh: 当钻石说它“依然蓝”时，它真正想表达什么？

options.en: It feels sad/down, not just its blue color
options.zh: 它感到难过/消沉，而非仅指颜色蓝

options.en: It wants to be repainted according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它想重新上色（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says, "But I am still blue, and I'm not talking about my color. I want to go to parties." The diamond uses 'blue' to mean feeling sad.
explain.zh: 书中说：“但我依然蓝，而我说的不是我的颜色。我想去参加派对。”钻石用“蓝”双关表示心情低落。

q.en: Why does the author have the diamond tell its own story in the first person?
q.zh: 作者为什么让钻石用第一人称讲述自己的故事？

options.en: To make hundreds of years of history feel personal, fun, and mysterious
options.zh: 为了让数百年的历史显得亲切、有趣又神秘

options.en: To list every king's name according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了罗列每位国王的名字（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The diamond says, "I do like to tell my story... How much of my story you believe is up to you," making history playful and engaging for the reader.
explain.zh: 钻石说：“我确实喜欢讲自己的故事……我的故事你信多少，由你决定。”这让历史对读者而言既俏皮又引人入胜。

q.en: What are the 'four Cs' used to sell diamonds?
q.zh: 卖钻石时所用的“4C”是什么？

options.en: Cake, Candy, Cookie, Cracker according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 蛋糕、糖果、曲奇、饼干（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says, "Diamonds are sold based on the four Cs: Carat... Color... Cut... Clarity."
explain.zh: 书中说：“钻石依据4C出售：克拉……颜色……切工……净度。”

prompt.en: The Hope Diamond tells its own story in a fun, first-person voice, sharing hundreds of years of history—from India to kings of France to a museum in Washington, D.C. If an object you own could talk, what story would it tell? What would it say about the people who owned it?
prompt.zh: 希望钻石用有趣的“第一人称”讲述自己的故事，跨越数百年历史——从印度到法国国王，再到华盛顿特区的博物馆。如果你的一件物品会说话，它会讲什么故事？它会怎样描述曾经拥有它的人？

en: The diamond was found in India and sold to France's king in 1668.
It was stolen in 1792 and cut down to about 45 carats.
Henry Philip Hope and later owners like Harry Winston cared for it.
Imagine an object's 'life story' and the people in it.
zh: 钻石发现于印度，1668年卖给法国国王。
亨利·菲利普·霍普及后来的哈里·温斯顿等拥有者珍视它。
想象一件物品的“人生故事”与其中出现的人。
